title: Minimum Operations to Make the Array K-Increasing
titleCn: 使数组 K 递增的最少操作次数

testcases.input: arr = [5,4,3,2,1], k = 1 Output: 4 Explanation: For k = 1, the resultant array has to be non-decreasing. Some of the K-increasing arrays that can be formed are [5,6,7,8,9], [1,1,1,1,1], [2,2,3,4,4]. All of them require 4 operations. It is suboptimal to change the array to, for example, [6,7,8,9,10] because it would take 5 operations. It can be shown that we cannot make the array K-increasing in less than 4 operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: 4 Explanation: For k = 1, the resultant array has to be non-decreasing. Some of the K-increasing arrays that can be formed are [5,6,7,8,9], [1,1,1,1,1], [2,2,3,4,4]. All of them require 4 operations. It is suboptimal to change the array to, for example, [6,7,8,9,10] because it would take 5 operations. It can be shown that we cannot make the array K-increasing in less than 4 operations.

testcases.input: arr = [4,1,5,2,6,2], k = 2 Output: 0 Explanation: This is the same example as the one in the problem description. Here, for every index i where 2 <= i <= 5, arr[i-2] <= arr[i]. Since the given array is already K-increasing, we do not need to perform any operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: This is the same example as the one in the problem description. Here, for every index i where 2 <= i <= 5, arr[i-2] <= arr[i]. Since the given array is already K-increasing, we do not need to perform any operations.

testcases.input: arr = [4,1,5,2,6,2], k = 3 Output: 2 Explanation: Indices 3 and 5 are the only ones not satisfying arr[i-3] <= arr[i] for 3 <= i <= 5. One of the ways we can make the array K-increasing is by changing arr[3] to 4 and arr[5] to 5. The array will now be [4,1,5,4,6,5]. Note that there can be other ways to make the array K-increasing, but none of them require less than 2 operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: Indices 3 and 5 are the only ones not satisfying arr[i-3] <= arr[i] for 3 <= i <= 5. One of the ways we can make the array K-increasing is by changing arr[3] to 4 and arr[5] to 5. The array will now be [4,1,5,4,6,5]. Note that there can be other ways to make the array K-increasing, but none of them require less than 2 operations.
